column3: "Garlic lowers blood pressure" refers to its ability to reduce hypertension as some active ingredients act to relax blood vessels and thus increase circulation, promoting healthy heart function.
"Garlic boosts immune system" refers to its ability to enhance body's defense mechanism against various pathogens, leading to better health.
"Garlic reduces inflammation" refers to its ability to ease the body's response to irritation, typically characterized by redness or swelling.
"Garlic fights infection and disease" refers to its natural antibacterial properties that help prevent or treat various illnesses.
"Garlic lowers cholesterol levels" refers to its ability to reduce blood fats, which may contribute to a healthier heart and lower risks of certain cardiovascular diseases.

"Garlic promotes healthy digestion" refers to its ability to improve stomach health by enhancing the function of the gastrointestinal tract, leading to better absorption and utilization of nutrients from food.
"Garlic prevents cancer cell growth" refers to its ability to hinder the development of malignant cells, which can lead to lower rates of cancer.

"Garlic supports cardiovascular health" refers to its ability to potentially improve blood flow, lower cholesterol levels, and reduce inflammation which are essential factors for maintaining a healthy heart and circulatory system.
"Garlic improves brain function" refers to its ability to enhance cognitive performance and memory retention due to its rich content of antioxidants, essential nutrients, and bioactive compounds.
"Garlic alleviates respiratory problems" refers to its ability to help ease breathing issues like asthma or bronchitis due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
